steps.title: Recognize the discriminant rule for real solutions
steps.content: A quadratic equation $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ has exactly one distinct real solution when the discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$ equals zero. This represents a parabola that touches the x-axis at exactly one point.

steps.title: Calculate the discriminant for each choice
steps.content:
For each equation, compute the discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$:

  • A: $x^2 - 16 = 0$ → $0^2 - 4(1)(-16) = 64 > 0$ (two solutions)
  • B: $x^2 + 16 = 0$ → $0^2 - 4(1)(16) = -64 < 0$ (no solutions)
  • C: $x^2 - 16x + 56 = 0$ → $(-16)^2 - 4(1)(56) = 32 > 0$ (two solutions)
  • D: $x^2 - 16x + 64 = 0$ → $(-16)^2 - 4(1)(64) = 0$ (exactly one solution)
That matches choice D — D is correct.
